profileRecord.recordDesc:
<@profile.key>CODENAME: Rossi
GENDER: Female
AUTHENTICATION: The Pack
DOB: March 10
RACE: Lupo
[ORIPATHY INFECTION STATUS]
Oripathy positive (infected), based on medical examination reports.

[INTEGRATED PHYSICAL EXAMINATION]
PHYSIOLOGICAL STRENGTH: Standard
COMBAT SKILL: Standard
TACTICAL ACUMEN: Excellent
ORIGINIUM ARTS ASSIMILATION: Excellent</>

<@profile.key>Operator Rossi, full name Rossina Wulfperl Luppino, is a member of a Landbreaker clan known as the Pack. As one of the clan's representatives, she has entered a cooperative partnership with Endfield Industries. Rossi now serves under the Specialist Tech Division.

Given the unique status of Operator Wulfgard, all collaborative affairs between Endfield Industries and the Pack are effectively focused upon Operator Rossi.
Rossi is not a permanent resident aboard Dijiang. She spends most of her time with the Pack and handling clan affairs. However, locating her is far less challenging than finding Wulfgard. She is also quite receptive to participating in some of Endfield Industries' training programs. Evaluations from Lil' Dodge states: "Exceptionally high tactical acumen. Possesses considerable leadership charisma and battlefield command talent."
Operator Rossi aligns more closely with public perception of what a member of the Pack should be: devoted to the family, conforms to the code, and respectful of their elders. On her very first day aboard Dijiang, she paid visits to nearly every department head as well as operators of considerable renown. Her well-mannered and conscientious demeanor left a strong impression on many.
However, if you were to face her in combat, you shall witness an entirely different side of Rossi: razor-sharp and utterly ruthless.
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

<@profile.key>Rossi's Observation Notes
Nonno Rozzan
Nonno Rozzan is always so quiet when he walks. Davvero strano ... he's so tall, yet his footsteps are as light as if he were walking on a thick carpet. I've almost never seen him slip up. No matter where I hide, he always finds me.
Every time I watch Nonno Rozzan handle his gun, it feels like time just ... si ferma. Even the sound of breathing disappears. I don't know how long it lasts before he finally lowers it. I've never actually seen him pull the trigger. He just wipes the barrel down with a cloth, puts it back in its case, and that's the end of training. I've tried to imitate him a few times, but ... I can't hold the stance for nearly as long as he does.
...
Catello
Catello's always in a hurry. Seriously, what's he so busy with? As for his training methods ... annoyingly simple. Mind-numbingly boring strength drills mixed with a few sets of dynamic target practice, or just straight to live combat exercises... Così noioso. Absolutely nothing worth learning from. Skipping this!
...
Sorella Arclight
Sorella Arclight can move across steep cliff faces so quickly. Is it just a matter of pure speed? I asked her, and she said it requires some Arts... She also meditates during her training. She says it helps her clear her mind of distractions. Ugh... Staying completely still for that long is way too hard for me.
...
Endministrator
The Endmin always adjusts to the pace of other teammates so everyone can keep up. Totally different from Catello! And the Endmin's training is mostly about manipulating Originium and turning the rocks into razor sharp weapons. How can anyone do that? Something only the Endmin can do, I guess. No point trying to learn it...</>

<@profile.key>Coming of Age? Within the Pack, everyone's rite of passage is different. For some, it's completing a solo hunt. For others, it's closing a complex business deal.
The Pack doesn't seem worried about these youngsters messing up. They boldly assign critical matters as trials to the young wolves, a practice that would seem utterly insane to anyone outside the Pack.
As for Rossina... Hunts and business deals? She'd already pulled those off at an even younger age. To her, they were just routine.
So the Pack arranged a special trial for her.
They left Rossina in a deserted village, with only five luckless bandits for company. Those outlaws were desperate men, each already eyeing whatever meager means of survival the others had left, just waiting for a chance to stab each other in the back.
The five of them kept each other in check, none daring to make the first move. But ... if that delicate balance was broken...
Rossina's first task was simply to survive the bandits. That wasn't enough on its own. Beast attacks were also frequent there. Sometimes a lone predator, sometimes an entire group.
The Elder Wolves dropped her off and left without a second thought. They seemed unconcerned whether she could survive in such an environment.
When they returned to the village two weeks later, the sight that greeted them left them stunned.
A fence had been erected around the village. The five bandits and Rossina were taking shifts on watch, vigilant against beast attacks. On days without raids, some of the five would go out hunting, others would gather fresh water. They had actually shaped into a proper, functioning team.
Those men once constantly viewed each other with suspicion. No one knows how Rossina got them to follow her lead. In her own words, she just "made them understand the only way to survive."
It's also worth mentioning that Catello went through the same trial. Well... He spent a few days killing all the nearby beasts, left enough food behind, then slipped away without anyone noticing. He only returned at the end of the trial.
— Interview Transcript with Pack associate, Marco Wulfhowl Luppino</>

<@profile.key>"The Pack sent a tiny pup to talk? Did old Rozzan and his crew kicked the bucket or something?"
Rossi glanced at Erik, who was roaring with laughter along with his crew, and instantly lost any interest she might have had in the leader famously known as the "Cannon of the Wildlands".
This was Rossi's first time handling the clan's ... "business". A week prior, a merchant convoy bound for the Luppino family had been intercepted by Erik. Out of respect for the Pack's name, they hadn't dared touch the couriers, but a sizable portion of the goods meant for return had gone missing. With Catello absent from the clan at the time, Rossi had volunteered, eager to prove her mettle with this particular "deal".
But at that moment, Rossi was starting to regret her decision. This trip was even less interesting than a hunting trip in home turf. The so-called "Cannon of the Wildlands" was wriggling his bulky, bloated body while slapping the table with his hand. From the moment she'd walked in, he'd done nothing but attempt to provoke her.
True strength is reserved, unyielding, never showing a crack. That was a lesson Rossi had learned within the clan. No one, not even Rozzan or the Elder Wolves, ever boasted so crudely about their violence.
"Nonno Rozzan told me this is 'business.' From where I stand, every deal has one side that profits, and another that takes the loss."
Rossi's eyes swept across the tent. The scent of gunpowder drifted to her nose, and she pinpointed where the other side had planted the explosives. The amount was small. Clearly, killing her wasn't the goal. A live wolf seemed far more useful to them.
"The Pack doesn't mind letting our business friends wet their muzzles a little. Unfortunately, I haven't passed my rite of passage yet, so I'm not a true member of the Pack. And I only want profit. I don't do losses."
"Looks like the Pack isn't planning to sit down and talk this out properly, eh?"
A flicker passed through Erik's eyes. The bandits around them grew serious, some already resting their hands on their weapons.
Two swordsmen, two axemen, and probably several casters lying in wait outside the tent. Judging by their positioning inside, it'd be tough to clear them out quickly. So—
Having assessed the situation, Rossi toyed with a fork on the table, waiting for the right moment.
"Listen. From the very first day I set foot in this blighted place, someone's been blabbering non-stop in my years. I can't do this. I can't kill that. I must follow the rules — the Code of the Pack."
Erik's agitation grew. He stared hard at the girl who'd come alone. All these preparations were meant for Rozzan of the Pack. But the old man sent a bratty girl instead... Erik felt insulted.
"The Code? It's written by those who are bigger and holds more power!"
Erik hefted his cannon. The Pack prized their kin. Taking this girl hostage could prove far more useful.
In a flash, a fork shot toward Erik. Rossi sprang from her seat and suddenly disappeared. Erik swatted the utensil away and raised his weapon to aim.
"Behave and be still."
A dagger pressed against the back of his neck.
The surrounding bandits finally drew their weapons, but it was too late. 
"STOP! DON'T GET ANY CLOSER!" Erik yelled, feeling a chilling prick of cold against the back of his neck.
"Counting on those casters at the door to bail you out? But that's odd ... it's way too quiet. Seems my clan wrapped things up even faster than I did. What were you saying again? Oh, right ... the Code..."
The icy tip of the blade seemed to press an inch closer.
"Do you understand what the Code of the Pack is?" Rossi sheathed her dagger.
Erik whirled around, furious. All his careful planning, ruined by a child like this? "I gave the goods to the Bonekrushers. Try and take them back if you have the guts for it! Once they've wiped you out, then I'll be writing up a new Code for this turf!"
"What did you say?"
A killing intent he had never felt before radiated from the girl standing before him.
Erik didn't know the price of bringing up the Bonekrushers around this girl, but he was about to learn. And soon.

Looking at the wrecked tent, Rossi couldn't help but sigh.
"Business is such a hassle."</>

<@profile.key>Three tolls of the bell sounded. One by one, the people at the gathering removed their hats and stepped forward to place fresh flowers into the cold coffin.
Some wept quietly, others bowed their heads in silence. Rozzan scanned the surroundings, but he didn't spot that familiar crimson figure anywhere.
The last of the Elder Wolves who survived the incident laid in the casket. He spent the rest of his days in pain. Though the Bonekrusher's haze ravaged his organs and their blades pierced his limbs, the Elder Wolf held on tightly to the swaddled young pup ... until he passed her into the arms of another boy...
"She's still not here..."
The words reached Rozzan. He gave a slow nod, then quietly passed the rest of the service over to another Elder.
He knew where to find her. Whenever she was upset, she'd lose herself in the training grounds and stay there until late into the night.
Rozzan watched the girl in silence. She noticed him too, but neither said a word. With one last fierce thrust, she drove her sword into the training dummy and collapsed to the ground. No one knew how long she had been pushing herself here.
"He's gone."
Rozzan approached Rossi where she lay gasping on the ground, his voice quiet and flat.
"I know... I know..."
Rossi pulled her hood down over her eyes, but her voice shook uncontrollably. Ever since she was old enough to understand, she had visited the wounded Old Wolf every single week so he could see that she was growing up strong and well. To her, he was the last living thread connecting her to those lost souls.
"You should have been there. He would have wanted you there."
"But ... it was because of me... He did it all for..."
Rozzan hoisted her up and slung her over his shoulder before she could finish, carrying her toward the courtyard where the funeral was being held.
"I can't face him ... it was all my fault... I don't want to..."
Rossi thrashed and bit against Rozzan's shoulder. Her nails scraped his skin, her teeth sank into his arm. Rozzan's expression didn't flicker. He just kept walking toward the funeral.
Soon, Rossi's strength gave out. She slumped against Rozzan's back, arms hanging limp.
"I didn't become the wolf the Pack could rely on..."
Rossi murmured, unsure if she was speaking to Rozzan, or to someone no longer there.
"I never learned to stand on my own. I couldn't lead anyone. I haven't done a single thing right... I didn't deserve their sacrifice..."
Rozzan came to a stop. The courtyard was empty now, save for the ashes of burnt offerings and a solitary coffin. Inside, the elder lay peacefully, covered in flowers.
"Every time you came to see him, he'd always pull me aside afterward to talk. Maybe age caught up to the wolf and he never remembered what he told me. Just kept ranting the same stories on and on."
Rozzan set Rossi down. He rested a hand on the edge of the coffin, gazing at his old friend.
"He'd always go on about the glory days, the few times we butted heads. But the details ... those kept changing. One day he was the one who messed up, the next it was me. The same story was told different every time. Only one thing he ever said never changed."
Rozzan reached over and ruffled Rossi's hair.
"Every single time, he'd say that saving you was the greatest thing he ever did."
Rossi lowered her head. She took off the pale pendant from around her neck, the one the elder had given her, and placed it among the flowers.
The coffin sealed. The young wolf never cried again.</>

profileVoice.voiceDesc: This is my chance to show Endmin what I'm made of!
I won't let anyone down!
Rossi from the Pack. E-Endmin... What's our mission?
I'll do my absolute best, Endmin!
Ah! This weapon is so cool! I've never seen anything like this back in the clan!
It's a bit hard to move in... Oh? You picked it out for me, Endmin? I'll take it then! Hmm... There must be some deeper meaning behind this...
I am a descendant of the Pack!
So that's how seasoned warriors fight... Hmm ... like this ... and then like that... Okay, I think I've got it now!
Pro ... motion? Ah, I get it! We have similar ceremonies back in the clan, though I've never actually experienced one myself. You know what? This isn't ... half bad.
Hehe, I'm doing pretty well, right? Gotta be this good to shoulder the clan's responsibilities. After all, I'm gonna make the Pack famous across Talos-II!
Promotions are a piece of cake for someone like me! When's the next one? I'm totally ready! Gotta wait for the right opportunity? Well ... alright then.
Doesn't this prove I'm the top operator now? Wonder if Catello had it this easy. Next time I see him, I'm totally rubbing it in his face!
I'm Rossina, granted name Wulfperl, meaning the Pack's treasure! You'd better remember that! Ah ... Endmin, you can call me whatever you prefer...
The hour of bloodshed is upon us. It's time to hunt. I mean ... it's time to eat.
Prey, beware! I am the paws of the Pack! ...Wait, that doesn't sound right? Anyway... Awoo!
Piece of cake for an elite like me!
E-Endmin! Do you have any missions for me today?
Endmin, could you share some leadership experience with me?
How does Endfield manage so many operators? Something about ... systems? And some kind of ... central control? I can't figure it out ... but it's definitely worth learning.
The elder wolves from the clan told me to visit Dijiang more often. They said I could learn about leadership here, especially by your side. But getting a chance to catch you is just so hard!

The big brothers and sisters on Dijiang always treat me like a kid! But I'm already an elite of the Pack. Next time I'll make it clear to them, this has to stop! ...Though the snacks they give me are really good ...

Clan affairs come first! But if anything urgent happens to Endfield, I'll definitely rush over right away. It's just ... I'm not sure if you really need my help... You're so capable, you could probably handle crises easily, even without me. But I still want to be helpful... What should I do...

I heard Catello could be found on Dijiang, but why's he so elusive here too? Hmm ... guess old habits die hard? If I can't find him soon, Nonno Rozzan's gonna be furious. I really don't wanna see him angry... Endmin, do you know where I might find Catello?

Why can't I find any records about you on Dijiang, either? I finally learned how to use this so-called Intel Archive, but all I get are useless results or "No Authority" messages... Ugh... Endmin, do you have time to tell me about your heroic deeds yourself?
Endmin, these are the spoils I've collected! They're all for you! Don't worry, I'll get even more!
This is ... for me? Can I really have it? Thank you! I'll make sure to understand the meaning behind your gift!
This? Well ... I'm just taking notes on your words and actions, Endmin. I think even the details are worth learning from. Ah ... please don't look! These are personal notes!

I-I've heard so many legends about you. I've always wondered what kind of person you'd be! But now that I've actually met you, I feel like you're even more admirable than the stories say. What exactly? Well ... your methods, your leadership... I-I don't even know how to put it into words...

Children of the Pack participate in the coming-of-age ceremony at thirteen. The clan gathers to choose a granted name for the new "wolf". Catello's name is "Wulfgard", and mine is "Wulfperl". I love the name the clan gave me. Every time I say it, I can feel the blood of the Pack flowing through me... What do you think of it, Endmin?
Endmin, I've been doing pretty well on missions lately, right? So ... could I maybe ask for a little reward? Ah ... nothing too fancy, just chatting with me more would be enough! ...Is that okay?

I know I'm not skilled enough yet to lead the Pack, but the elders insist I handle the clan's affairs... So I want to learn more by your side, Endmin. I want to learn how to become someone trustworthy like you, and Perlica, and M3... I'll make everyone see me in a new light!

The big brothers and sisters at Endfield have taught me a lot! Perlica explained how Protosyncs work, Avywenna took me to TGCC's clothing and jewelry stores, and ... and Xaihi said she wants to discuss quadratic time complexity with me... I don't even know what that means.

The big brothers and sisters here all take good care of me. They even give me gifts. But I've passed the Pack's trials! I'm a real wolf! A mature wolf shouldn't like childish things. They should prefer daggers and coins! So I've collected plenty of those. Once I grow a bit taller, everyone can finally trust me with the most dangerous missions!

I always thought Nonno Rozzan was the strongest person in the world, but he kept saying "there's always someone greater" and mentioned you all the time, Endmin. Later, I heard tons of legendary stories about you elsewhere. And I was like, "Wow, we actually got someone like you out there?!" I've always wanted to see what you're really like. Ever since I heard you woke up, I've been planning to surprise you one day ... hehe.

My first dagger was a reward after an emergency hunt! The box it came in was all shiny and golden, I've kept it safe ever since. This one's my favorite too. I always carry it with me, though it's not very sharp, good mostly for cutting fruit. If you'd like it, Endmin ... well ... I'll give it to you once I bring the box along too!

About my childhood... The Pack was always caught in conflicts with the Bonekrushers. It was my brother who took me around seeking medical help, and the clan members also... Oh! Ah ... I shouldn't be talking about this in front of you! Forget it, Endmin. Let's talk about you instead! I've heard that...

"Originium will carve a path for us." Ah... I-I was just practicing... Oh, no, no... Will you hate me for this? I know learning from you isn't just about copying... I just thought ... it sounded really cool...

As a Pack member, I could use my granted name as a codename like my brother does, but ... I haven't really been called that by anyone outside the clan yet, so it might feel a bit embarrassing. And "Rossina" just sounds too childish ... no way I'm using that! I ended up going with "Rossi" as my codename, after the color of my cloak.

I won't let a single one of those Bonekrusher brutes get away! This is a blood feud between the Pack and the Bonekrushers. Too many wolves, too many family members, died at their hands just to save me. This is the Pack's vengeance, and mine too. We're the clan that holds grudges longest in this world, and one day, we will settle this score!

It's stained with the blood of my kindred ... but to me, every time I touch it, I feel an incredible sense of peace, as if they never really left. This cloak burns, reminding me never to forget the vendetta, but it also gives me courage, and the warmth of family... Yes, they're always with me.

I used to keep all the daggers and coins I collected in a special treasure box, but I lost it during the move… What's worse is it had some really embarrassing stuff inside — crafts I made as a kid. It took me forever to find the box again. Now I can store everything safely on Dijiang! Better yet, I can use storing things as an excuse to hang out with the big brothers and sisters here. No … I mean, to study here!

Everything people do in the wilderness is just to survive. Sometimes I saw kids my age doing terrible things just to get by... Sometimes even Landbreakers took desperate risks simply to stay alive. I think everyone should be able to live by their own choices. Endmin, can Endfield change all this, so those kids can pursue their dreams like I do?
Trust me, something good is over there!
The Pack never overlooks any resources in the wild. Keep an eye on that area!
There's a strange smell over there. Let's go check it out!
Smells like danger. Should we engage?
Can you spare a few of those stones for me? I want to inlay them into my dagger.
Let's do this together. We'll finish this work in no time!
Hmm ... I was thinking if it won't open, maybe I could try biting it.
Why do some Aurylene float around while others just stay still? It's marvelous.
All done! No more of that annoying Blight!
Eat well, sleep well, rest well. That's the wisdom of the Pack!
Quick, use these to recover!
I feel much better now!
Careful! Something's coming!
No time ... to lick my wounds now...
The Pack ... won't let this slide...
Nice hit!
Whoa! Perfect teamwork!
Hehehe, I've got better tricks up my sleeve!
This is the fight we want!
The blood of the Pack is boiling!
Wouldn't wanna miss out on this hunt!
The name of the Pack will echo across the land!
Learned your lesson? This is what happens when you mess with the Pack!
Heh-heh-heh. With me around, victory is a sure thing!
They bit deep, but the Pack isn't afraid of more scars!
This little setback means nothing... I, Rossina won't give up!
Get ripped!
Tremble!
Tear them apart!
Hunting time!
Scorching claws!
We'll hunt them down!
I'll make them fear!
No escape!
I got you!
Down you go!
Awoo!
Seethe, blood of the Pack!
The blade is me!
Wolf spirits, stand with me.

charBreak40.description: Activate this to raise operator level cap to 60
charBreak40.name: Promotion II

equipBreakT4.description: Activate this to let the operator equip gold quality gear
equipBreakT4.name: Outfitting III

equipBreakT3.description: Activate this to let the operator equip purple quality gear

chr_0028_wulfa_ComboSkill.desc:
COMBO TRIGGER: When an enemy has both <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> and <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> stacks.
Rossi's combo skill has 2 consecutive sequences. COMBO SEQUENCE 1 deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to the target.
COMBO SEQUENCE 2 <#ba.consume>consumes</> all <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> stacks from the target, deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> based on the number of Arts Infliction stacks <#ba.consume>consumed</>, applies 1 instance of <#ba.airborne>Lift</>, and buffs Rossi's Critical Rate and Critical DMG Dealt at the same time.
If COMBO SEQUENCE 2 is cast with <@ba.key>perfect timing</>, it applies 1 more stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>.
chr_0028_wulfa_ComboSkill.name: Moment of Blazing Shadow

chr_0028_wulfa_NormalAttack.desc:
BASIC ATTACK: 
An attack with up to 5 sequences that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. As the controlled operator, <#ba.lastcombo>Final Strike</> also deals <@ba.poise>{poise:0}</> Stagger. 

DIVE ATTACK: 
Basic attack performed in mid-air becomes a dive attack that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to nearby enemies. 

FINISHER: 
Basic attack performed near a Staggered enemy becomes a finisher attack that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and recovers some SP.
chr_0028_wulfa_NormalAttack.name: Seething Wolfblood

chr_0028_wulfa_NormalSkill.desc:
Charges to the target to deal <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and apply <#ba.airborne>Lift</>.
If the target already has <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stack(s), the skill also unleashes a <@ba.key>Wolven Ambrage</> that charges at the target to deal <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>.
chr_0028_wulfa_NormalSkill.name: Crimson Shadow

chr_0028_wulfa_UltimateSkill.desc:
Rossi flutters her cape and unleashes a flurry of stabs that quickly deals multiple sequences of <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> to the target. She then performs a two-sequence slash attack with her dagger that deals massive <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> and applies <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>.
<@ba.info>When this skill successfully scores a critical hit, it deals higher Critical DMG.</>
chr_0028_wulfa_UltimateSkill.name: "Razorclaw" Ambuscade

itemTable.decoDesc:
Rossi's employment contract.
"If you disrespect the most treasured gem of the Pack, you disrespect everyone in the Pack."
itemTable.desc: Rossi is a Sword-wielding guard who deals Physical DMG.
itemTable.name: Rossi

chr_0028_wulfa_potential_1.desc:
Battle skill <@ba.key>Crimson Shadow</> and combo skill <@ba.key>Moment of Blazing Shadow</> improved: The DMG Multipliers of these skills are increased to <@ba.vup>{atk_scale_3:0.00}</> times of the original.
<@ba.key>Wolven Ambrage</> of the battle skill also <#ba.return>returns</> <@ba.vup>{atb_return:0}</> SP after hitting the enemy.

chr_0028_wulfa_potential_4.desc: Ultimate <@ba.key>"Razorclaw" Ambuscade</> improved: Ultimate Energy cost <@ba.vup>-{1-costvalue:0%}</>.

chr_0028_wulfa_potential_5.desc: Ultimate <@ba.key>"Razorclaw" Ambuscade</> improved: DMG Multiplier is increased to <@ba.vup>{potential_5_damage_scale:0.0}</> times of the original; Critical DMG Dealt is increased by <@ba.vup>+{potential_5_critical_damage:0%}</>.

chr_0028_wulfa_talent_2_1.desc: When Rossi's skill deals Critical DMG to a <#ba.rossi>Razor Clawmarked</> enemy, trigger another hit that deals <@ba.vup>{bleed_critical_damage_scale:0%}</> ATK (Rossi's) of <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> and restores <@ba.heal>[Intellect × {heal_scale:0.00}]</> HP to Rossi. If the target is also <#ba.burning>Combusted</>, then the aforementioned DMG and HP Restoration effects are increased to <@ba.vup>{talent2_burning_damage_scale:0.0}</> times.

chr_0028_wulfa_potential_3.desc: Talent <@ba.key>Seething Blood</> improved: When triggered, it grants Base DMG Multiplier <@ba.vup>+{bleed_critical_damage_scale:0%}</> and restores HP <@ba.vup>+[Intellect × {heal_scale:0.00}]</>.

chr_0028_wulfa_talent_1_1.desc:
Battle skill improved: After <@ba.key>Wolven Ambrage</> deals DMG, Rossi applies <#ba.rossi>Razor Clawmark</> to the target for <@ba.vup>{duration_bleed:0}</>s. This effect cannot stack.
For the duration of <#ba.rossi>Razor Clawmark</>, the target takes <@ba.vup>{atk_scale_bleed:0%}</> ATK (Rossi's) of <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> per second and suffers <@ba.pd>Physical DMG Taken</> and <@ba.fire>Heat DMG Taken</> <@ba.vup>+{damage_up:0%}</>.

chr_0028_wulfa_talent_1_2.desc:
Battle skill improved: After <@ba.key>Wolven Ambrage</> deals DMG, Rossi applies <#ba.rossi>Razor Clawmark</> to the target for <@ba.vup>{duration_bleed:0}</>s. This effect cannot stack.
For the duration of <#ba.rossi>Razor Clawmark</>, the target takes <@ba.vup>{atk_scale_bleed:0%}</> ATK (Rossi's) of <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> per second and suffers <@ba.pd>Physical DMG Taken</> and <@ba.fire>Heat DMG Taken</> <@ba.vup>+{damage_up:0%}</>.

chr_0028_wulfa_talent_2_2.desc: When Rossi's skill deals Critical DMG to a <#ba.rossi>Razor Clawmarked</> enemy, trigger another hit that deals <@ba.vup>{bleed_critical_damage_scale:0%}</> ATK (Rossi's) of <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> and restores <@ba.heal>[Intellect × {heal_scale:0.00}]</> HP to Rossi. If the target is also <#ba.burning>Combusted</>, then the aforementioned DMG and HP Restoration effects are increased to <@ba.vup>{talent2_burning_damage_scale:0.0}</> times.

Bonus DMG Multiplier per Infliction Stack
Bonus DMG Multiplier per Infliction Stack
Bonus DMG Multiplier per Infliction Stack

spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_1_1.desc: Assign to Reception Room to slow Mood Drop of all operators in it by 14%
spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_1_1.name: Clan Etiquette β

spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_1_2.desc: Assign to Reception Room to slow Mood Drop of all operators in it by 18%
spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_1_2.name: Clan Etiquette γ

spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_2_1.desc: Assign to Reception Room to grant operator clue collecting efficiency +20%
spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_2_1.name: Scent of the Hunt β

spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_2_2.desc: Assign to Reception Room to grant operator clue collecting efficiency +30%
spaceship_skill_chr_0028_wulfa_2_2.name: Scent of the Hunt γ

tag_expert_sense.desc:
Situational Analysis: Clan Diplomat
Being able to sniff out a scam is a dangerous talent for a child.

tag_expert_street.desc:
Street Wisdom: Conflict Mediator
Rossi is tired of endless bickering. So, she mastered the means of getting both sides to shut up.

tag_hobby_nature.desc:
Nature: Fantasies for Heroism
A weird-looking stone and a sharp chisel have been selected. Now, who should this figurine look like?

tag_hobby_exercise.desc:
Sports: Game of the Hunt
Tracking down a prey is a long process, but Rossi is very patient. No need to rush, for we are close to the scent of blood.

item_pic_1_chr_0028_wulfa.name: Cherished Little Perl
item_pic_1_chr_0028_wulfa.author: Yukuso
item_pic_1_chr_0028_wulfa.description: Getting this unlocks Rossi's snapshot: "Cherished Little Perl". Operator snapshots can be showcased on a loop using the EF02 Slide Projector in the Reception Room aboard Dijiang.
item_pic_1_chr_0028_wulfa.decoDescription: A well-cuddled and much loved fruit grew in protecting hands. It transformed nourishment into a solid exterior and an incorruptible heart.

item_pic_3_chr_0028_wulfa.name: Unlimited Future
item_pic_3_chr_0028_wulfa.author: Whitebear
item_pic_3_chr_0028_wulfa.description: Getting this unlocks Rossi's snapshot: "Unlimited Future". Operator snapshots can be showcased on a loop using the EF02 Slide Projector in the Reception Room aboard Dijiang.
item_pic_3_chr_0028_wulfa.decoDescription: A king ... a warrior ... a movie star! I really wish to be them all if I get to choose! Ahem... It's just a wish of mine.

item_pic_5_chr_0028_wulfa.name: A World at the Tip of the Pen
item_pic_5_chr_0028_wulfa.author: Yukuso
item_pic_5_chr_0028_wulfa.description: Getting this unlocks Rossi's snapshot: "A World at the Tip of the Pen". Operator snapshots can be showcased on a loop using the EF02 Slide Projector in the Reception Room aboard Dijiang.
item_pic_5_chr_0028_wulfa.decoDescription: The canvas recorded the legends and stories. Plus the castle from last night's dream and the little wolf pup who chased away the disaster.

0.desc: Guards are skilled at making enemies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</> and applying <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Statuses</>. They are also good damage dealers.
0.name: Guard

2.desc: Defenders are extremely tough. They are skilled in protecting and treating their teammates, as well as counterattacking the enemy.
2.name: Defender

4.desc: Supporters are skilled in applying various control effects that weaken the enemy. They can also buff and support their teammates.
4.name: Supporter

5.desc: Casters are skilled at applying <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Inflictions</> and <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reactions</>. They are also good damage dealers.
5.name: Caster

7.desc: Great at recovering Skill Points (SP) to help their teammates' skill casting.
7.name: Vanguard

8.desc: Strikers exploit Physical or Arts effects inflicted by other operators to deal decisive damage.
8.name: Striker

tag_power_syndicates.tagName: United Workers' Syndicates of Talos II

adv.electricFence.desc: An active high voltage fence is impassable. Follow the cable, find the switch, and shut down the fence for safe passage.
adv.electricFence.name: High Voltage Fence

fac.hub.desc: The Protocol Automation-Core (PAC) is the central facility of the Automated Industry Complex (AIC) Factory. Storage management becomes available when standing in the Core AIC Area of the PAC.
fac.hub.name: Protocol Automation-Core

adv.eswitch.desc: A reactive device changes its operation mode when attacked or hit by an explosion. It is often used to switch off <#adv.electricFence>High Voltage Fences</> or activate <#adv.jumpMachine>Bounce Devices</>.
adv.eswitch.name: Reactive Device

ba.spellvul.desc: Arts Susceptible targets temporarily take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.spellvul.name: Arts Susceptibility

item.quartzGlass.desc:
A fiber produced by refining <#item.quartzSand>Amethyst Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.quartzGlass.name: Amethyst Fiber

adv.jumpMachine.desc:
The controlled operator can use Bounce Devices to jump across a longer distance or reach high places unreachable with normal jumping.
Simply stand on a powered Bounce Device, then jump to use it.
adv.jumpMachine.name: Bounce Device

adv.powerTerminal.desc: An electric nexus is usually placed at the end of a power transmission line. Connect it to the power grid to supply power to nearby facilities. Electric Nexuses are often used to power gates and switches. Supply power to Electric Nexuses with Relay Towers. This then grants control access to gates or switches linked to them.
adv.powerTerminal.name: Electric Nexus

ba.crystenhance.desc: Cryo Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.crystenhance.name: Cryo Amp

ba.airborne.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then add 1 stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>, and deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</> and <@ba.poise>Stagger</>.
ba.airborne.name: Physical Status: Lift

item.bbflowerPowder.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.bbflower>Aketine</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.bbflowerPowder.name: Aketine Powder

ba.burning.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.burning>Combustion</> takes <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> over time.
<@ba.burning>Combustion</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Heat Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>
ba.burning.name: Arts Reaction: Combustion

ba.crush.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then <#ba.consume>consume</> all <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks and deal massive <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</>.
ba.crush.name: Physical Status: Crush

ba.burningonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.burning>Combusted</>: Temporarily take continuous <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> that ignores their DEF.
ba.burningonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Combustion

ba.enhance.desc: Amped targets deal more DMG of the element it is Amped for. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.enhance.name: Amp

item.bbflowerSeed.desc:
Seeds picked from <#item.bbflower>Aketine</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Aketine Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Aketine.
Alternatively, Aketine Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilBbflower>Aketine Plots</>.
item.bbflowerSeed.name: Aketine Seed

ba.combo.desc: Next battle skill or ultimate cast by the team consumes the Link buff and deals more DMG, with battle skills getting a higher increase. Effects of the same type can stack, but with diminishing returns.
ba.combo.name: Link

item.procBattery1.desc:
A battery produced by packaging <#item.glassCmpt>Amethyst Part</> and <#item.originiumPowder>Originium Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
item.procBattery1.name: LC Valley Battery

ba.conduct.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.conduct>Electrification</> takes more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>.
<@ba.conduct>Electrification</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Electric Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</>
ba.conduct.name: Arts Reaction: Electrification

ba.conductonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.conduct>Electrified</>: Temporary stun and take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</> for a certain duration.
ba.conductonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Electrification

ba.physicalstatus.desc: Physical Statuses refer to physical debuffs applied to the enemy. They include: <#ba.airborne>Lifted</>, <#ba.knockdown>Knocked Down</>, <#ba.crush>Crushed</>, and <#ba.fracture>Breached</>.
ba.physicalstatus.name: Physical Status

ba.consume.desc: Various Physical and Arts debuffs can be ended early. For example, <#ba.crush>Crush</> consumes <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> debuffs, while <#ba.burning>Combustion</> consumes <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> debuffs.
ba.consume.name: Debuff Consumption

ba.poiseknot.desc: Some powerful enemies have <@ba.poise>Stagger Nodes</>. Deal enough <@ba.poise>Stagger</> to reach the <@ba.poise>Stagger Node</> to interrupt such enemies and make them stumble.
ba.poiseknot.name: Stagger Node

ba.statuslevel.desc: Status Level is the number of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> stacks (max 4) <#ba.consume>consumed</> when triggering a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reaction</>. The higher the <#ba.statuslevel>Status Level</>, the stronger the resulting <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> or <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reaction</>.
ba.statuslevel.name: Status Level

ba.corrupt.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> gradually loses resistance against all elements. 
<@ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Nature Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</>
ba.corrupt.name: Arts Reaction: Corrosion

item.plantMossSeed1.desc:
Seeds picked from <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Buckflower Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Buckflowers. Alternatively, Buckflower Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilMoss1>Buckflower Plots</>.
item.plantMossSeed1.name: Buckflower Seed

ba.corruptonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.corrupt>Corroded</>: Cannot dash and suffer movement speed reduction for a certain duration.
ba.corruptonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Corrosion

ba.crystbreak.desc: To an enemy with active <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, applying <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> effects will <#ba.consume>consume</> the <#ba.frozen>Solidification</> to trigger Shatter that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>.
ba.crystbreak.name: Shatter

ba.fireenhance.desc: Heat Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.fireenhance.name: Heat Amp

ba.rossi.desc: Rossi's battle skill applies a special status on the enemy. The affected target will take <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> over time, and suffers higher Physical DMG Taken and Heat DMG Taken.
ba.rossi.name: Razor Clawmark

ba.speedup.desc: Temporarily raises the movement speed of a target. The number represents the degree of the speed increase. Effects of the same type can stack until hitting the speed cap.
ba.speedup.name: Haste

ba.crystburst.desc: <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> + <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.cryst>Cryo Burst</> that deals <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</> to enemies.
ba.crystburst.name: Arts Burst: Cryo

ba.crystinflict.desc:
<@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.crystburst>Cryo Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Cryo Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> = <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>
ba.crystinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Cryo

item.plantMoss3.desc:
A plant that is characterized by rough, gritty leaves. It can be gathered from the wilderness.
The plant can be grown with AIC Factory facilities or cultivated with <#item.portSoilMoss3>Sandleaf Plots</>.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be shredded to produce <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</>.
item.plantMoss3.name: Sandleaf

ba.crystonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.frozen>Solidified</>: Cannot move for a certain duration.
ba.crystonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Cryo Infliction

ba.spelldmg.desc:
Damage can be divided into <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>, <@ba.key>Arts DMG</>, and <@ba.ether>Æther DMG</>.
<@ba.key>Arts DMG</> can be further divided into <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>, <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>, <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>, and <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>.
ba.spelldmg.name: DMG Type

ba.crystvul.desc: Cryo Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.cryst>Cryo DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.crystvul.name: Cryo Susceptibility

ba.dispel.desc: This removes specific debuffs from the operator.
ba.dispel.name: Dispel

item.ironPowder.desc: A powder produced by shredding <#item.ironNugget>Ferrium</> with AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to produce other materials.
item.ironPowder.name: Ferrium Powder

ba.dot.desc: DMG over time cannot trigger critical hits.
ba.dot.name: DMG Over Time

item.bottledRecHp1.desc:
A capsule made by <#sys.manualCraft>crafting</> or filling <#item.plantMossPowder1>Buckflower Powder</> into an <#item.glassBottle>Amethyst Bottle</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Use this to restore an operator's HP. It can also be equipped as a <#sys.tacticalItem>tactical</>.
item.bottledRecHp1.name: Buck Capsule [C]

ba.fireburst.desc: <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> + <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.fire>Heat Burst</> that deals <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> to enemies.
ba.fireburst.name: Arts Burst: Heat

ba.fireinflict.desc:
<@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.fireburst>Heat Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Heat Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> = <#ba.burning>Combustion</>
ba.fireinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Heat

ba.fireonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.burning>Combusted</>: Temporarily take continuous <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</> that ignores their DEF.
ba.fireonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Heat Infliction

ba.firevul.desc: Heat Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.fire>Heat DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.firevul.name: Heat Susceptibility

ba.fracture.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then <#ba.consume>consume</> all <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks, deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</>, and temporarily increase the <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> taken by the enemy.
ba.fracture.name: Physical Status: Breach

ba.pulseonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.conduct>Electrified</>: Temporary stun and take more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</> for a certain duration.
ba.pulseonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Electric Infliction

item.plantSpSeed1.desc:
Seeds produced by seed-picking <#item.plantSp1>Reed Rye</> plants with AIC Factory facilities.
Take Reed Rye Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</>, then go to the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen to craft them into <#item.portSoilSp1>Reed Rye Plots</>.
item.plantSpSeed1.name: Reed Rye Seed

sys.manualCraftField.desc: <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</> lets you craft planting plots with plant seeds and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</>. Planting plots can be placed and grown at the Eco-Farm.
sys.manualCraftField.name: Planting

ba.frozen.desc:
An enemy with <@ba.frozen>Solidification</> cannot move, and applying <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> or a <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> to the enemy triggers the <#ba.crystbreak>Shatter</> effect. 
<@ba.frozen>Solidification</> = A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Cryo Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>
ba.frozen.name: Arts Reaction: Solidification

item.plantMossSpc1.desc:
The Firebuckle is a plant that grows in the wilderness and is known for its exceptionally brilliant flowers.
There is a chance of getting Firebuckles when harvesting <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> in the wilderness.
They can be used for crafting <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>.
item.plantMossSpc1.name: Firebuckle

ba.frozenonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.frozen>Solidified</>: Cannot move for a certain duration.
ba.frozenonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Solidification

item.plantMossPowder1.desc:
A powder made by <#sys.manualCraft>crafting</> or shredding <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Use this to restore an operator's HP. It can also be equipped as a <#sys.tacticalItem>tactical</>.
item.plantMossPowder1.name: Buckflower Powder

ba.guard.desc: Protected targets temporarily take reduced DMG. The number represents the degree of reduction. When there are multiple effects of the same type, only the most powerful applies.
ba.guard.name: Protect

ba.knockdown.desc: Applies <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> on an enemy. If the enemy is already <#ba.noguard>Vulnerable</>, then add 1 stack of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</>, and deal <@ba.phy>Physical DMG</> and <@ba.poise>Stagger</>.
ba.knockdown.name: Physical Status: Knock Down

ba.lastcombo.desc: Final Strike is the last sequence of basic attack. When the controlled operator's Final Strike hits the enemy, it also deals <@ba.poise>Stagger</> and restores some Skill Points (SP).
ba.lastcombo.name: Final Strike

ba.naturalburst.desc: <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> + <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.natur>Nature Burst</> that deals <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> to enemies.
ba.naturalburst.name: Arts Burst: Nature

ba.naturalenhance.desc: Nature Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.naturalenhance.name: Nature Amp

item.plantMossSeed3.desc:
Seeds picked from <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaves</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Sandleaf Seeds can then be planted and grown at AIC Factory facilities to acquire Sandleaves.
Alternatively, Sandleaf Seed and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> can be used in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen and crafted into <#item.portSoilMoss3>Sandleaf Plots</>.
item.plantMossSeed3.name: Sandleaf Seed

item.portSoilBbflower.desc:
Aketine Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.bbflower>Aketine</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.bbflowerSeed>Aketine Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Aketine.
item.portSoilBbflower.name: Aketine Plot

ba.naturalinflict.desc:
<@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.naturalburst>Nature Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Nature Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_pulse" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> = <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>
ba.naturalinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Nature

ba.spellstatus.desc: <@ba.key>Arts Reactions</> are Arts debuffs that can be applied to the enemy. They include: <#ba.burning>Combustion</>, <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>, <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, and <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</>.
ba.spellstatus.name: Arts Reaction

sys.manualCraftDish.desc:
Fine Food & Meds (Fine F&M) can be used as <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>. 
Unlike <#sys.manualCraftTonic>Emergency Food & Meds</>, they can only be made using <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</>.
sys.manualCraftDish.name: Fine Food & Meds

ba.naturalonchar.desc:
Certain enemy skills can apply stacks of <@ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</> to operators. 
At 4 stacks, the operator becomes <@ba.corrupt>Corroded</>: Cannot dash and suffer movement speed reduction for a certain duration.
ba.naturalonchar.name: Operator Debuff: Nature Infliction

ba.naturalvul.desc: Nature Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.naturalvul.name: Nature Susceptibility

ba.pulseinflict.desc:
<@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> is a type of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> can be applied and stacking triggers <#ba.pulseburst>Electric Burst</>. 
A <#ba.spellinflict>non-Electric Infliction</> (<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_fire" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_energy_fusion_cryst" scale=1.25>/<image="BuffIcon/icon_infliction_nature" scale=1.25>) + <@ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> = <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>
ba.pulseinflict.name: Arts Infliction: Electric

ba.noguard.desc:
Hitting an enemy for the first time with a skill with <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Status</> applies <@ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> instead of the actual Physical Status.
A <@ba.phy>Vulnerable</> enemy can be <#ba.airborne>Lifted</> and <#ba.knockdown>Knocked Down</> to add more Vulnerability stacks (up to 4). Hitting a Vulnerable enemy with <#ba.crush>Crush</> or <#ba.fracture>Breach</> will <#ba.consume>consume</> the Vulnerability stacks.
ba.noguard.name: Vulnerable

sys.manualCraft.desc:
Open the Backpack panel to access the Crafting screen. Different raw materials can be crafted into useful items.
For example, <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflower</> can be crafted into <#item.plantMossSeed1>Buckflower Powder</> for restoring HP.
sys.manualCraft.name: Crafting

ba.originium.desc: The Endministrator's combo skill attaches Originium Crystals to enemies and immobilizes them.
ba.originium.name: Originium Crystal

ba.physicalenhance.desc: Physical Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.physicalenhance.name: Physical Amp

ba.physicalvul.desc: Physical Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.physicalvul.name: Physical Susceptibility

item.glassBottle.desc:
A bottle produced by moulding <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.glassBottle.name: Amethyst Bottle

ba.pulseburst.desc: <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> + <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> will trigger <@ba.pulse>Electric Burst</> that deals <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> to enemies.
ba.pulseburst.name: Arts Burst: Electric

ba.slow.desc: Slowed targets temporarily suffer movement speed reduction. The number represents the degree of reduction. When there are multiple effects of the same type, only the most powerful applies.
ba.slow.name: Slow

ba.pulseenhance.desc: Electric Amped targets temporarily deal more <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.pulseenhance.name: Electric Amp

item.originiumEnrPowder.desc:
A fine, dense powder produced by grinding <#item.originiumPowder>Originium Powder</> and <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.originiumEnrPowder.name: Dense Originium Powder

ba.pulsevul.desc: Electric Susceptible targets temporarily take more <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.pulsevul.name: Electric Susceptibility

item.glassCmpt.desc:
A part produced by fitting <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.glassCmpt.name: Amethyst Part

ba.return.desc:
After spending SP and meeting certain criteria, some of the SP spent will be returned. 
SP Return is not "SP Recovery" and does not activate SP Recovery-triggered effects. Spending returned SP also does not give Ultimate Energy.
ba.return.name: SP Return

ba.shield.desc: The shield can absorb damage while it is active. Some shields may only absorb specific type(s) of DMG.
ba.shield.name: Shield

ba.spellburst.desc: <@ba.key>Arts Bursts</> are triggered by stacking <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Inflictions</> of the same elements. They include <#ba.fireburst>Heat Burst</>, <#ba.pulseburst>Electric Burst</>, <#ba.crystburst>Cryo Burst</>, and <#ba.naturalburst>Nature Burst</>.
ba.spellburst.name: Arts Burst

item.originiumPowder.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.originium>Originium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.originiumPowder.name: Originium Powder

ba.spellenhance.desc: Arts Amped targets temporarily deal more <#ba.spelldmg>Arts DMG</>. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.spellenhance.name: Arts Amp

ba.spellinflict.desc:
<@ba.key>Arts Inflictions</> are special Arts debuff inflicted upon the enemy. There are 4 elements of Arts Inflictions: <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>, <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</>, <#ba.crystinflict>Cryo Infliction</>, and <#ba.naturalinflict>Nature Infliction</>. 
Up to 4 stacks of <@ba.key>Arts Infliction</> of the same element can be applied.
ba.spellinflict.name: Arts Infliction

ba.spellinflictonchar.desc:
Some enemy skills can apply stacks of <#ba.burningonchar>Heat Infliction</>, <#ba.conductonchar>Electric Infliction</>, <#ba.frozenonchar>Cryo Infliction</>, or <#ba.corruptonchar>Nature Infliction</>.
At 4 stacks of the Arts Inflictions, the operator becomes <#ba.burningonchar>Combusted</>, <#ba.conductonchar>Electrified</>, <#ba.frozenonchar>Solidified</>, or <#ba.corruptonchar>Corroded</> respectively.
ba.spellinflictonchar.name: Arts Inflictions / Arts Reactions on Operators

ba.vulnerable.desc: Susceptible targets temporarily take more DMG of the element it is Susceptible to. The number represents the degree of increase. Effects of the same type can stack.
ba.vulnerable.name: Susceptible

ba.weak.desc: Weakened targets temporarily deal reduced DMG. The number represents the degree of reduction. Effects of the same type can stack non-linearly.
ba.weak.name: Weaken

item.bbflower.desc:
A plant that contains flammable and explosive substances. It can be gathered from the wilderness.
The plant can be grown with AIC Factory facilities or cultivated with <#item.portSoilBbflower>Aketine Plots</>.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be shredded to produce <#item.bbflowerPowder>Aketine Powder</>.
item.bbflower.name: Aketine

item.carbonMtl.desc:
A material produced by refining <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflowers</> with AIC Factory facilities.
Carbon is essential for crafting <#sys.manualCraftField>planting</> plots, and can also be used for producing other materials.
item.carbonMtl.name: Carbon

item.plantMoss1.desc:
The Buckflower is a plant that grows in the wilderness and is known for its brightly colored flowers.
The plant can also be grown with AIC Factory facilities or <#item.portSoilMoss1>Buckflower Plots</>.
The plant can be used for crafting <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</> or other materials.
item.plantMoss1.name: Buckflower

item.crystalShell.desc:
A crystalline crust produced by refining <#item.originium>Originium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.crystalShell.name: Origocrust

item.ironEnrPowder.desc:
A fine, dense powder produced by grinding <#item.ironPowder>Ferrium Powder</> and <#item.plantMossPowder3>Sandleaf Powder</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.ironEnrPowder.name: Dense Ferrium Powder

item.ironNugget.desc: A metal ingot produced by refining <#item.ironOre>Ferrium Ore</> with AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to produce other materials.
item.ironNugget.name: Ferrium

item.ironOre.desc:
An ore that can be gathered from Ferrium Ore mining spots in the wilderness.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.ironNugget>Ferrium</>.
item.ironOre.name: Ferrium Ore

item.originium.desc:
An ore that can be gathered from Originium Ore mining spots in the wilderness. It can be used for power generation in the AIC Factory.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.crystalShell>Origocrust</>.
item.originium.name: Originium Ore

item.plantMossPowder3.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaves</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials, usually by grinding.
item.plantMossPowder3.name: Sandleaf Powder

item.plantSp1.desc:
The Reed Rye is a type of plantable crop gatherable from the wilderness. It can also be acquired by cultivating <#item.portSoilSp1>Reed Rye Plots</>.
It can be directly used for crafting <#sys.manualCraftDish>Fine Food & Meds</> (Fine F&M).
item.plantSp1.name: Reed Rye

item.portSoilMoss1.desc:
Buckflower Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantMoss1>Buckflower</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantMossSeed1>Buckflower Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the <#sys.manualCraftField>Planting</> screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot has a higher chance of getting bonus <#item.plantMossSpc1>Firebuckle</>.
item.portSoilMoss1.name: Buckflower Plot

item.portSoilMoss3.desc:
Sandleaf Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantMoss3>Sandleaf</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantMossSeed3>Sandleaf Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Sandleaves.
item.portSoilMoss3.name: Sandleaf Plot

item.portSoilSp1.desc:
Reed Rye Plots can be cultivated at the Eco-Farm to acquire <#item.plantSp1>Reed Rye</>.
These plots can be crafted by using <#item.plantSpSeed1>Reed Rye Seed</> and <#item.carbonMtl>Carbon</> in the Planting screen.
Harvesting a fully grown plot provides plenty of Reed Rye.
item.portSoilSp1.name: Reed Rye Plot

item.quartzPowder.desc:
A powder produced by shredding <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</> with AIC Factory facilities.
It can be used to produce other materials.
item.quartzPowder.name: Amethyst Powder

sys.tacticalItem.desc:
Tacticals are items that can be used during combat to provide various benefits and buffs. Every tactical has different activation criteria and usage limits.
Every operator can be equipped with 1 tactical. During battle, whenever the tactical activation criteria are met, the operator will auto-use their equipped tactical.
After expending all tactical usage attempts in a single battle, one more use will be restored after a while.
sys.tacticalItem.name: Tacticals

item.procBomb.desc:
Industrial Explosives are a type of explosive that can be produced.
They are made by using the Packaging Unit, an AIC Factory facility. The materials required are <#item.glassCmpt>Amethyst Part</> and <#item.bbflowerPowder>Aketine Powder</>.
Use Industrial Explosives to deal DMG to enemies or take down destructible objects within the blast radius.
item.procBomb.name: Industrial Explosive

item.quartzSand.desc:
An ore that can be gathered from Amethyst mining spots in the wilderness.
It can also be processed with AIC Factory facilities. For example, it can be refined to produce <#item.quartzGlass>Amethyst Fiber</>.
item.quartzSand.name: Amethyst Ore

sys.manualCraftTonic.desc:
Emergency Food & Meds (EMS F&M) can be used as <#sys.tacticalItem>tacticals</>.
They can be acquired by <#sys.manualCraft>Crafting</> or by using AIC Factory facilities.
sys.manualCraftTonic.name: Emergency Food & Meds

list.attributeHint:
Arts Intensity influences bonuses to <#ba.physicalstatus>Physical Statuses</>, <#ba.spellstatus>Arts Reactions</>, and <#ba.spellburst>Arts Bursts</>.
· When triggered, <#ba.crush>Crush</>, <#ba.burning>Combustion</>, <#ba.frozen>Solidification</>, and <#ba.crystbreak>Shatter</>, <#ba.spellburst>Arts Burst</> deal DMG <@ba.vup>+{Damage:0%}</>.
· When triggered, <#ba.fracture>Breach</>, <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>, and <#ba.corrupt>Corrosion</> deal DMG <@ba.vup>+{Damage:0%}</> and gain Effect Enhancement +<@ba.vup>{Debuff:0%}</>.
· When triggered, <#ba.airborne>Lift</> and <#ba.knockdown>Knock Down</> deal DMG <@ba.vup>+{Damage:0%}</> and Stagger <@ba.vup>+{Poise:0%}</>.
list.name: Arts Intensity
